pathway_summary:
A C. elegans neuron-to-intestine endocrine circuit that couples serotonin/octopamine neuronal signaling to intestinal lipolysis. Serotonin from ADF (tph-1) acts on URX (mod-1), which relays via octopamine (RIC/tbh-1) and ser-6 (AWB) and ultimately sends an endocrine signal to the intestine, where nhr-76 induces ATGL (atgl-1) to drive beta-oxidation and fat loss. Boundaries: starts at neuronal serotonin biosynthesis/sensing, ends at intestinal lipolysis/fat loss; upstream feeding/nutrient cues and the molecular identity of the endocrine signal are out of scope.</parameter>
<parameter name="genes">[
  {"symbol": "tph-1", "mf_class": "enzyme", "note": "Tryptophan hydroxylase in ADF; rate-limiting serotonin (5-HT) biosynthesis enzyme producing the signal sent to URX."},
  {"symbol": "ser-6", "mf_class": "signaling_receptor", "note": "Serotonin/octopamine GPCR in AWB; receives octopamine from RIC and relays signal toward ADF."},
  {"symbol": "mod-1", "mf_class": "ligand_gated_channel", "note": "Serotonin-gated chloride channel in URX; direct receptor for 5-HT from ADF, drives the downstream endocrine signal."},
  {"symbol": "tbh-1", "mf_class": "enzyme", "note": "Tyramine beta-hydroxylase in RIC; produces octopamine (Oct) acting on AWB/ser-6."},
  {"symbol": "nhr-76", "mf_class": "nuclear_receptor", "note": "Intestinal nuclear hormone receptor; transcriptionally induces ATGL/atgl-1."},
  {"symbol": "ATGL", "mf_class": "enzyme", "note": "Adipose triglyceride lipase (figure label); intestinal lipase driving beta-oxidation and fat loss. Map to atgl-1 in C. elegans."},
  {"symbol": "atgl-1", "mf_class": "enzyme", "note": "C. elegans ortholog of ATGL shown in figure; same node — use atgl-1 as the canonical gene for the lipase activity."}
]</parameter>
<parameter name="molecules">[
  {"label": "5-HT", "role": "direct_activator", "acts_on": "mod-1", "directness_note": "Serotonin is produced by tph-1 in ADF and directly gates the mod-1 serotonin-gated chloride channel in URX — a bona fide receptor ligand (RO:0012001). Producer(tph-1)->consumer(mod-1) relay."},
  {"label": "5-HT", "role": "pathway_output", "acts_on": "tph-1", "directness_note": "Same molecule as the output (has_output) of the tph-1 tryptophan hydroxylase activity in ADF; the relay's producing end."},
  {"label": "Oct", "role": "direct_activator", "acts_on": "ser-6", "directness_note": "Octopamine produced by tbh-1 in RIC; directly binds the ser-6 GPCR in AWB as its ligand (RO:0012001). Producer(tbh-1)->consumer(ser-6) relay."},
  {"label": "Oct", "role": "pathway_output", "acts_on": "tbh-1", "directness_note": "Octopamine is the has_output of the tbh-1 (tyramine beta-hydroxylase) activity in RIC; producing end of the relay."},
  {"label": "endocrine signal", "role": "upstream_stimulus", "acts_on": null, "directness_note": "Unidentified secreted signal from URX (dashed arrow, granules drawn) reaching the intestine; molecular identity unknown, so model as causal flow/stimulus rather than a direct activator of nhr-76. Flag uncertainty."}
]</parameter>
</invoke>
